summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering zoning text amendments for shipping containers, acceptance of an AED donation, scheduling a special meeting for fire levy discussion, and approval of ARPA funds for construction of a pole barn at the service department. The board also approved a replat of six lots into one to facilitate the building permit, declared surplus items for sale, and entered executive session regarding personnel matters.
decisions: Motion to amend the agenda (revised item L for $74,950 ARPA appropriation) – passed
Motion to adopt the agenda as amended – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (May 15 through June 11, 2024) – passed
Zoning case ZC-2024-005 text amendments regarding shipping containers and accessory structures approved – passed
Motion to accept donation of AED from CPAAA for administration building – passed
Motion to schedule special Board of Trustees meeting on June 17 at 7:30 PM – passed
Motion to cancel the June 25 regular meeting – passed
Motion to appoint Trustee Allen and Administrator Rose to Tax Incentive Review Council (TIRC) meeting August 7 – passed
Motion to increase appropriations of $48,000.20 for police body worn camera contract renewal – passed
Motion to declare surplus and sell on GovDeals.com 28 vehicle back seats and old analog phones – passed
Motion to accept donation of two decorative rocks from Bob Hauserman for Goshen Cemetery – passed (Trustee Hauserman abstained)
Motion to approve Resolution OR-10-2024 replat of lots 1-6 of Town of Goshen creating lot 1A – passed
Motion to appropriate $74,950 into ARPA service department account for pole barn construction – passed
Motion to adjourn into executive session at 6:31 PM – passed
Motion to adjourn regular meeting at 6:56 PM – passed
action_items: Advertise the special meeting on June 17 at 7:30 PM and the cancellation of the June 25 meeting
Trustee Allen and Administrator Rose to attend TIRC meeting on August 7 from 1-3 PM
Chief to prepare fire levy resolution of necessity for the June 17 special meeting
Resolution to proceed for fire levy scheduled for July 16 meeting
Service department to proceed with pole barn construction using Mullins Maintenance and WES Electric
Surplus 28 vehicle back seats and old analog phones to be listed on GovDeals.com
Chief Myers to continue work with Conger and architect on fire station estimate (expected in approximately 4 weeks)
key_amounts: $74,950 appropriated from ARPA funds for pole barn construction
$48,000.20 increased appropriation for police body worn camera contract renewal
$25,950 estimated cost for concrete for pole barn
$37,570 estimated cost for the pole barn building
Approximately $110,000 remaining in ARPA service department funds after pole barn appropriation
$2.5 million potential state funding for fire station on fast track in Columbus
$250,000 referenced as cost of service trucks sitting outside without shelter
notable_topics: Zoning text amendments for shipping containers as accessory structures (ZC-2024-005)
AED donation from CPAAA for administration building
Fire station funding update – $2.5 million state funding on fast track
Construction of 5-bay pole barn (80x31) at service department using ARPA funds
Replat of six lots into one for building permit purposes
Police body worn camera contract renewal
Special meeting scheduled for June 17 for fire levy discussion
Tax Incentive Review Council (TIRC) representative appointments
Surplus disposal of vehicle back seats and analog phones
notes: The pole barn project has been discussed since Station 19 was built; the old dilapidated barn was torn down in 2017. The public meeting on the fire levy is scheduled for 6:00 PM on June 17, with the special trustees meeting to follow at 7:30 PM. Trustee Hauserman abstained from the vote on accepting his own donation of decorative rocks to the cemetery. The meeting was notably short at approximately 31 minutes before executive session.
title: BOT 6-11-24
